meaning: adj.公平的,无偏见的,没有成见的
examples: He leaned over backwards to prove he was unprejudiced.
We must be honest and unprejudiced as we attempt to analyze it.
Our perceptive faculties grow quiet, unprejudiced, and unacquisitive.
And we mean to have the news always honest, intelligent and unprejudiced.
Those who really understand the situation, including the 12 unprejudiced foreign banks that do business in Malaysia, realise it is a well-run country with good prospects.
